arts.ttl: France U21 captain Enzo Millot available at cut-price
arts.sum: Enzo Millot (22) has enjoyed an excellent start to the season. His form for VfB Stuggart has not only seen him named captain of the France U21 side, and L’Équipe…

arts.ttl: Jamal Musiala to miss Bayern Munich’s game vs VfB Stuttgart
arts.sum: Speaking at his pre-match press conference (via @iMiaSanMia) ahead of Bayern Munich’s game vs VfB Stuttgart, head coach Vincent Kompany has provided an update on Jamal Musiala. Musiala had missed…

arts.ttl: Bayer Leverkusen chief unsure where things went wrong between Bayern Munich, Jonathan Tah
arts.sum: I can’t judge how the decisions work at Bayern,” Carros told the Phrasenmäher podcast (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ). Bayer Leverkusen CEO Fernando Carro did not want center-back Jonathan Tah to make the jump to Bayern Munich last summer, but he knows that things were getting very close to a transfer.

arts.ttl: Friday's Transfer Talk Update: Alphonso Davies, Luke Shaw, Frenkie de Jong
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up the latest confirmed transfer news and speculation from across the footballing world.

arts.ttl: Enzo Millot’s low exit clause revealed
arts.sum: Enzo Millot (22) has been involved in seven goals across nine games for Stuttgart this season and it’s since been revealed that the Frenchman has a low exit clause. As…

arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Did FC Barcelona fall for the Nigerian Prince email?; Leon Goretzka wants to stay and fight at Bayern Munich; Real Madrid wants Arsenal star; Liverpool scoping Jeremie Frimpong; Manchester City could chase Theo Hernández; and MORE!
arts.sum: When former Bayern Munich star Robert Lewandowski transferred to FC Barcelona, he could not imagine that he would be embroiled in an international scandal. Goretzka, Palhinha, and Konrad Laimer could, indeed, be needed at some point this season, but it does appear that the club — at least for now — has decided that Kimmich and Pavlović look like the immediate future for the position in Bavaria.

arts.ttl: Frans Krätzig talks life at Stuttgart, working with former Bayern Munich colleague Sebastian Hoeneß, competition with Mittelstädt and more...
arts.sum: After a breakout 2023/24 season in which he went from strength to strength and proved himself on some big stages, Bayern Munich ’s Frans Krätzig has rather gone under the radar during his loan spell at VFB Stuttgart, with the outside world only aware of his scant appearances off the bench for Die Schwäben in his several months there. Is part of that challenge working with VFB Stuttgart head coach or is it an advantage that Sebastian Hoeneß, who also has a past at the FC Bayern campus and is familiar with how training is organised there?

arts.ttl: Max Eberl denies executive interference, wants to prove he is not a “one-hit wonder” at Bayern Munich
arts.sum: But Bayern executive board member for sport Max Eberl does not see it that way. But there have been public statements from Hoeneẞ — notably, when the honorary president warned, “Max Eberl and Christoph Freund know full well that no one else will be signed if one or two prominent players don’t leave.”

arts.ttl: Manchester City keeper Éderson reveals why he was glad that Harry Kane joined Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Speaking to ESPN Brasil, Manchester City goalkeeper Éderson admitted that Bayern Munich striker Harry Kane was the toughest he’s ever faced. “Man, I’ve faced a few strikers, especially in the Premier League,” said the Brazilian.

arts.ttl: Bavarian Podcast Works — Weekend Warm-up Show Season 4, Episode 14: Talking Bayern Munich and MORE!
arts.sum: Bayern Munich is back in action and — like always — there is a lot to talk about with the team. The Bavarians have a big match with VfB Stuttgart, but there are a lot of high-level subjects that have rushed to the forefront of what fans are debating, so let’s get to it!
